intro: Blakea calycularis, also known as the 'Blakea' or 'Rainforest tree', is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Blakea calycularis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
